_meta.purpose: Layer-3 of the citation system: maps each evergreen coverage guide to its HONEST primary source. Coverage-scope claims must NOT cite rate filings (overclaim) — they cite standard policy-form language, DOI consumer guidance, or statute. See EEAT_PLAN.md §3 Layer 3.
_meta.status: PARTIAL — Opus 4.8, updated 2026-07-20. The 5 SHIPPED guides (renters theft, renters water-damage, does-car-insurance-cover, home-required, renters-required) now have REAL, WebFetch-VERIFIED secondary URLs (III / FEMA-NFIP / CFPB / Texas DOI) rendered inline; each `url_verified` records the confirming quote + date. Remaining 13 entries keep `doi_or_consumer_url: TODO` — those guides don't yet carry the Layer-3 form note (deferred per content-first). NO URLs fabricated: every filled URL was fetched and confirmed to support its claim.
_meta.citation_convention: Inline at the claim, muted. Pattern: 'Under the standard ISO HO-3 homeowners form, <claim> — your carrier's form may differ.' The 'may differ' caveat is itself a trust signal. For statutory claims, cite the state statute/DOI bulletin, not a form.

standard_forms.home: ISO HO-3 (form number HO 00 03) — the special-form homeowners policy most owner-occupied policies build on (open-peril dwelling, named-peril contents).
standard_forms.renters: ISO HO-4 (form number HO 00 04) — the contents/liability renters form.
standard_forms.auto: ISO Personal Auto Policy (form number PP 00 01).

home.form_ref: ISO HO-3 — covered: sudden & accidental discharge/overflow from plumbing/appliances; excluded: gradual seepage/leakage over weeks/months, and flood (separate NFIP/private policy).
secondary: NAIC consumer guidance on water vs. flood

home.form_ref: ISO HO-3 — flood is EXCLUDED; coverage requires NFIP or private flood policy.
ISO HO-3 — mold generally excluded UNLESS resulting from a covered water loss; policies commonly cap mold remediation (a sub-limit) and many require a specific endorsement to raise it.
secondary: state DOI mold bulletin (several states publish one)

home.form_ref: ISO HO-3 — roof damage covered when caused by a covered peril (wind/hail); wear-and-tear/age excluded. Watch for ACV-roof / cosmetic wind-hail endorsements that reduce payout.

ISO HO-3 — a tree that falls and strikes a covered structure from a covered peril is covered; debris-removal is capped (commonly ~$500-$1,000); a tree that falls without hitting a structure is usually not covered.

ISO HO-3 — settling, cracking, and earth movement are EXCLUDED; foundation damage is covered only when it results from a covered peril (e.g., a sudden plumbing discharge). Earthquake needs a separate policy/endorsement.

ISO HO-3 — wind and hail are covered perils; coastal policies frequently carry a separate percentage hurricane/wind deductible, and some coastal areas exclude wind (covered via a state wind pool/FAIR plan).
ISO HO-3 — water/sewer backup is EXCLUDED by default; covered only if a water-backup / sump-overflow endorsement is added (typically a modest sub-limit).
ISO HO-3 Section II (personal liability) — dog-bite injury to others is generally covered, subject to breed exclusions and prior-bite exclusions that many carriers apply.
secondary: state statute on strict liability / one-bite rule

home.form_ref: NOT required by law; required contractually by mortgage lenders. Statutory/lender basis, not a policy-form claim.
home.url_verified: 2026-07-20 — CFPB: lenders generally require proof of homeowners insurance (contractual, not statutory)

home.form_ref: ISO HO-3 — Section I (dwelling/other structures/contents/loss-of-use) + Section II (liability/med-pay); open-peril dwelling, named-peril contents.

renters.form_ref: ISO HO-4 — personal-property theft is a covered named peril, on- and (subject to sub-limits) off-premises.
renters.url_verified: 2026-07-20 — confirms theft covered, off-premises incl. property stolen from a car, floods excluded

renters.form_ref: ISO HO-4 — personal property stolen FROM a vehicle is covered under the renters policy (off-premises contents), NOT under auto; the car itself and permanently-installed parts fall to auto comprehensive.
ISO HO-4 — sudden/accidental water discharge to personal property covered; flood excluded (contents flood via NFIP).
renters.url_verified: 2026-07-20 — FEMA/NFIP: standard renters insurance excludes flood; NFIP contents coverage available to renters

renters.form_ref: NOT required by law; commonly required by the LEASE/landlord. Contractual, not statutory.
renters.url_verified: 2026-07-20 — Texas DOI: 'Renters insurance isn't required by law. Some landlords might require you to have a renters policy.'

renters.form_ref: ISO HO-4 — personal property (named perils), personal liability, loss-of-use/additional living expense; does NOT cover the building structure.

auto.form_ref: ISO PP 00 01 — liability (BI/PD), medical payments/PIP, uninsured/underinsured motorist, and physical damage (collision + comprehensive/other-than-collision).
auto.url_verified: 2026-07-20 — III lists liability (BI/PD), MedPay/PIP, UM/UIM, collision, comprehensive

auto.form_ref: ISO PP 00 01 — coverage generally follows the CAR (permissive-use), with some coverages following the named insured; permissive-use and exclusions are the nuance.
auto.confidence: medium — verify state-by-state nuance before citing as absolute
